title: Henriettella parviflora
intro: Henriettella parviflora, also known as Pterocaulon virgatum, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory activities. The bioactive constituents of Henriettella parviflora include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and boost the immune system.
